message.content:
Implement the following plan: # Plano: Widget Elementor — BIT Dropdown Button ## Contexto A página `/publicacoes/` usa botões "DOWNLOAD" com dropdown construídos manualmente via widget HTML do Elementor. O resultado é inconsistente: alguns itens do JetEngine listing têm a estrutura HTML completa (botão estilizado com borda, ícone, dropdown) enquanto outros exibem texto simples "DOWNLOADS" sem nenhum estilo. Converter o componente para um **widget Elementor nativo** resolve a inconsistência e permite controle visual direto no painel do Elementor. --- ## Estado atual **`bit-dropdown-btn.php`** — apenas enfileira o CSS: ``` add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', fn() => wp_enqueue_style('bit-dropdown-btn', ...)) ``` **`bit-dropdown-btn.css`** — estilos via CSS variables (`--btn-normal-*`). O ícone usa `mask-image: var(--ucpa-icon-download)` — variável **não definida** em `style.css` (é o motivo do ícone ausente/inconsistente em alguns botões). **HTML atual (manual no widget HTML):** ```html
PT EN
``` --- ## Referência: padrão do projeto Ver `bit-elementor-svg-widget.php` — o padrão de widget já estabelecido: - Classe definida dentro do hook `elementor/widgets/register` - `register_controls()` com tabs Content e Style - `selectors` nos controles de cor escrevem CSS variables no `{{WRAPPER}}` para reuso dos estilos do CSS file --- ## Implementação ### Arquivo 1 — `bit-dropdown-btn.php` (expandir) **Adicionar** classe `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` dentro de `elementor/widgets/register`, mantendo o `wp_enqueue_scripts` existente. **Controls — Tab Content:** | Controle | Tipo | Default | |---|---|---| | `label` | TEXT | `DOWNLOAD` | | `links` | REPEATER | — | | `links > item_label` | TEXT | `Português` | | `links > item_url` | URL | — | | `links > item_target` | SELECT | `_self` / `_blank` | **Controls — Tab Style:** Todos os controles usam `selectors` apontando para `{{WRAPPER}}` com CSS variables — o CSS file cuida do resto: | Controle | CSS variable | |---|---| | `btn_width` | SLIDER → `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | `color_bg` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-bg: {{VALUE}}; }` | | `color_txt` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-txt: {{VALUE}}; }` | | `color_bdr` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-bdr: {{VALUE}}; }` | | `color_bg_hv` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-bg-hv: {{VALUE}}; }` | | `color_txt_hv` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-txt-hv: {{VALUE}}; }` | | `color_icn` | COLOR → `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-icn: {{VALUE}}; }` | **Render:** ```php protected function render() { $s = $this->get_settings_for_display(); $label = esc_html($s['label'] ?: 'Download'); $links = $s['links'] ?? []; // Gerar HTML com sanitização } ``` O ícone SVG (seta para baixo dentro de círculo) será embutido inline no PHP (dados do SVG direto em `render()`), eliminando a dependência de `--ucpa-icon-download`. ### Arquivo 2 — `bit-dropdown-btn.css` (ajuste) - Remover o comentário que referencia `--ucpa-icon-download` (linha 9) - Remover as regras `.dropdown-btn-icon` que usam `mask-image: var(--ucpa-icon-download)` — o ícone passa a ser SVG inline renderizado pelo widget - Manter tudo o mais (layout, hover, dropdown menu) --- ## Arquivos modificados | Arquivo | Ação | |---|---| | `mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` | Adicionar widget Elementor (mantém CSS enqueue) | | `mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.css` | Remover regras `mask-image` do ícone (agora inline) | Após implementação, copiar ambos para `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/` (padrão do projeto). --- ## Verificação 1. No Elementor, buscar widget "BIT Download Button" na barra lateral — deve aparecer 2. Inserir widget em qualquer página → verificar preview com label e links configurados 3. Acessar `/publicacoes/` — os botões existentes (HTML widget) continuam funcionando (CSS não mudou) 4. Testar hover: dropdown aparece, cores mudam, ícone renderiza 5. Testar controles de estilo no painel do Elementor: alterar cor de background → reflete no preview If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/dcambria/.claude/projects/-Users-dcambria-scripts-server-tools-v2-docker-dev-sites-concertacao/b18c9018-aebb-47ff-96df-2b609646442a.jsonl

file.content:
<?php
/**
 * Plugin Name: BIT Dropdown Button
 * Description: Componente dropdown button para a página de Publicações.
 *              CSS isolado do child theme — depende das variáveis --btn-normal-*
 *              e --ucpa-icon-download definidas no child theme (style.css :root).
 *              HTML via widget HTML Elementor: div.dropdown-btn-wrapper >
 *              div.dropdown-btn-container > button.dropdown-btn-toggle +
 *              div.dropdown-btn-menu.
 * Version:     1.0.0
 * Author:      Bureau IT
 * Network:     true
 */

if ( ! defined( 'ABSPATH' ) ) {
    exit;
}

add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', function () {
    wp_enqueue_style(
        'bit-dropdown-btn',
        WPMU_PLUGIN_URL . '/bit-dropdown-btn.css',
        [ 'hello-elementor-child' ],
        filemtime( WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR . '/bit-dropdown-btn.css' )
    );
} );

'(retorna /en quando WPML estiver em inglês). ' . 'ATENÇÃO: no site www-concertacao.com.br preencher manualmente com ' . 'https://concertacaoamazonia.com.br (domínio diferente).', 'ai' => [ 'active' => false ], 'condition' => [ 'link_enabled' => 'yes' ], ] ); $this->add_control( 'link_target', [ 'label' => 'Abrir link em', 'type' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::SELECT, 'options' => [ '_self' => 'Mesma aba', '_blank' => 'Nova aba', ], 'default' => '_self', 'condition' => [ 'link_enabled' => 'yes' ], ] ); $this->end_controls_section(); // ── Cor genérica (para SVGs com fill:currentColor) ── $this->start_controls_section( 'style_section', [ 'label' => 'Cor', 'tab' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::TAB_STYLE, ] ); $this->end_controls_section(); // ── Tab Style ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── $this->start_controls_section( 'style_section', [ 'label' => 'Estilo', 'tab' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::TAB_STYLE, ] ); $this->add_control( 'btn_width', [ 'label' => 'Largura do botão', 'type' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::SLIDER, 'size_units' => [ 'px', '%' ], 'range' => [ 'px' => [ 'min' => 100, 'max' => 500, 'step' => 5 ], '%' => [ 'min' => 20, 'max' => 100, 'step' => 5 ], ], 'default' => [ 'unit' => 'px', 'size' => 220 ], 'selectors' => [ '{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle' => 'width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}};', ], ] ); $this->add_control( 'color_txt_hv', [ 'label' => 'Texto (hover)', 'type' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::COLOR, 'selectors' => [ '{{WRAPPER}}' => '--btn-normal-txt-hv: {{VALUE}};', ], ] ); $this->add_control( 'color_icn', [ 'label' => 'Ícone', 'type' => \Elementor\Controls_Manager::COLOR, 'selectors' => [ '{{WRAPPER}}' => '--btn-normal-icn: {{VALUE}};', ], ] ); $this->end_controls_section(); } private function get_download_icon_svg(): string { return ''; } protected function render() { $s = $this->get_settings_for_display(); $label = esc_html( ! empty( $s['label'] ) ? $s['label'] : 'Download' ); $links = ! empty( $s['links'] ) ? $s['links'] : []; echo '

<?php
/**
 * Plugin Name: BIT Dropdown Button
 * Description: Componente dropdown button para a página de Publicações.
 *              CSS isolado do child theme — depende das variáveis --btn-normal-*
 *              e --ucpa-icon-download definidas no child theme (style.css :root).
 *              HTML via widget HTML Elementor: div.dropdown-btn-wrapper >
 *              div.dropdown-btn-container > button.dropdown-btn-toggle +
 *              div.dropdown-btn-menu.
 * Version:     1.0.0
 * Author:      Bureau IT
 * Network:     true
 */

if ( ! defined( 'ABSPATH' ) ) {
    exit;
}

add_action( 'wp_enqueue_scripts', function () {
    wp_enqueue_style(
        'bit-dropdown-btn',
        WPMU_PLUGIN_URL . '/bit-dropdown-btn.css',
        [ 'hello-elementor-child' ],
        filemtime( WPMU_PLUGIN_DIR . '/bit-dropdown-btn.css' )
    );
} );

content.text:
Implementação concluída. Resumo do que foi feito:

**`bit-dropdown-btn.php` (v1.0.0 → v2.0.0):**
- Manteve o `wp_enqueue_scripts` existente
- Adicionou classe `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` dentro do hook `elementor/widgets/register`
- **Tab Content:** controle `label` (TEXT) + repeater `links` com `item_label` (TEXT) e `item_url` (URL com suporte a `is_external`/`nofollow`)
- **Tab Style:** `btn_width` (SLIDER), 6 controles de cor mapeando para CSS variables via `selectors` no `{{WRAPPER}}`
- Ícone: SVG inline (círculo + seta download) via `get_download_icon_svg()` — sem dependência de `--ucpa-icon-download`

**`bit-dropdown-btn.css` (v1.0.0 → v2.0.0):**
- Removidas as regras `mask-image` e `background-color` do bloco `.dropdown-btn-icon`
- Substituído por `color: var(--btn-normal-icn)` com `inline-flex` — o SVG usa `currentColor`
- Tudo o mais (layout, hover, dropdown menu) mantido intacto

**Ambos copiados para `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/`** conforme o padrão do projeto.

Here are the complete findings: ### Exact File Paths 1. **Widget Class**: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/jet-elements/includes/addons/jet-elements-download-button.php` 2. **Download Handler (Encryption/Decryption)**: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/jet-elements/includes/class-jet-elements-download-handler.php` 3. **Render Template**: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/jet-elements/templates/jet-download-button/global/index.php` 4. **CSS Styling**: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/jet-elements/assets/css/addons/jet-download-button.css` 5. **Base Class**: `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/jet-elements/includes/base/class-jet-elements-base.php` --- ### Encryption/Obfuscation Mechanism **Type**: SHA1 hash-based ID encryption with server-side mapping **Process**: 1. **Encryption** (`encrypt_id()` at line 138-148): - Computes: `sha1(NONCE_SALT . $id)` - Creates hash-to-ID mapping stored in WP option `jet_elements_download_button_hashes` - Returns the SHA1 hash as the encrypted ID 2. **Nonce Key** (`get_nonce_key()` at line 42-57): - Uses `NONCE_SALT` constant if defined - Falls back to generated key stored in `jet_elements_download_button_encrypt_key_start` option - Security key is created once and persists 3. **Decryption** (`decrypt_id()` at line 155-158): - Looks up hash in `jet_elements_download_button_hashes` option - Returns actual attachment ID or false if hash not found 4. **URL Generation** (`get_download_link()` at line 166-175): - Adds query parameter: `?jet_download=[sha1_hash]` - Example: `https://example.com/?REDACTED...` 5. **Download Processing** (`process_download()` at line 198-264): - Runs on `init` hook at priority 99 - Checks `$_GET['jet_download']` parameter - Decrypts hash to get attachment ID - Validates post is attachment type - Serves file with proper headers (Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Content-Length) - Supports chunked download (1MB chunks) to avoid memory issues **Security Features**: - Direct attachment IDs are never exposed in URLs - Hash-to-ID mapping is server-side only - Admins can reset hashes via WordPress admin panel (line 67-102) --- ### Widget Class Structure **Class Name**: `Jet_Elements_Download_Button` **Inheritance**: Extends `Jet_Elements_Base` (custom Elementor base class) **Key Methods**: | Method | Purpose | |--------|---------| | `get_name()` | Returns widget slug: `'jet-download-button'` | | `get_title()` | Returns "Download Button" | | `get_icon()` | Returns `'jet-elements-icon-download-button'` | | `get_categories()` | Returns `['cherry']` (Elementor category) | | `register_controls()` | Registers all Elementor panel controls | | `_format_label()` | Replaces `%size%` placeholder with file size | | `render()` | Renders widget by including template | **Controls** (Settings): | Control | Type | Description | |---------|------|-------------| | `download_file` | TEXT | Media library attachment ID (supports dynamic values) | | `download_id_encryption` | HIDDEN | Encryption flag (always enabled) | | `download_label` | TEXT | Main button text (default: "Download", supports %size% placeholder) | | `download_sub_label` | TEXT | Subtext (default: "%size%") | | `download_file_name` | SWITCHER | Show/hide filename | | `download_link_rel` | SWITCHER | Add `rel="nofollow"` attribute | | `download_icon` | ICON | Icon (supports Font Awesome, custom SVG) | | `download_icon_position` | SELECT | Icon position: left/right/top/bottom | --- ### Render HTML Structure **Template File** (index.php, lines 1-51): ```html [ICON_HTML] [Formatted Label with %size%] [Filename if enabled] [Formatted Sub-label] ``` **CSS Classes**: | Class | Purpose | |-------|---------| | `.jet-download` | Main button container (inline-flex) | | `.jet-download-icon-position-[left\|right\|top\|bottom]` | Icon position modifier | | `.jet-download__icon` | Icon wrapper | | `.jet-download__text` | Text container (label + sublabel) | | `.jet-download__label` | Main download text | | `.jet-download__sub-label` | Secondary text (usually file size) | | `.jet-download__file-name` | Filename display | **Icon Positioning**: CSS flexbox handles reordering via `order` property: - `left` (default): Icon order 1, Text order 2 - `right`: Icon order 3, Text order 2 - `top`: Icon order 1, Text order 2 (flex-direction: column) - `bottom`: Icon order 3, Text order 2 (flex-direction: column) --- ### Available Hooks & Filters **For Template Customization**: ```php apply_filters( 'jet-elements/download-button/css-scheme', [...] ) ``` **For Label Formatting**: ```php apply_filters( 'jet-elements/download-button/label', $text, $id ) ``` **For Control Modification**: ```php apply_filters( "jet-elements/editor/jet-download-button/include-controls", [] ) apply_filters( "jet-elements/editor/jet-download-button/exclude-controls", [] ) ``` **Download Process Hooks**: - `init` (priority 99): Download handler processes `?jet_download` parameter - `init` (priority 99): Hash reset handler processes admin reset requests --- ### Key Implementation Details 1. **File Download** (lines 217-263): - Clears all output buffers - Disables zlib compression - Sets proper MIME types (PDF, ZIP, JPG, force-download) - Streams file in 1MB chunks to memory-safe - Dies after complete download 2. **File Size Display**: - Uses `get_file_size()` which calls `get_attached_file()` → `filesize()` → `size_format()` - Placeholder `%size%` replaced in both label and sublabel 3. **Dynamic Content Support**: - `download_file` control has `'dynamic' => array( 'active' => true )` - `download_label` also supports dynamic values - Allows conditional rendering based on custom fields/postmeta 4. **Security**: - No direct file path exposure in URLs - Hash expires/resets when admin clicks reset link - Validates file exists before streaming - Checks post type is 'attachment' --- This is a production-grade download widget that securely obfuscates attachment IDs and provides extensive customization options for styling and labeling.

Sim, é plenamente possível — e é a abordagem ideal. Veja como funciona:

## Como a criptografia do JetElements funciona

O `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` armazena um mapa `sha1_hash → attachment_id` nas opções do WP. A URL gerada é `/?jet_download=HASH`. O handler processa no hook `init` e serve o arquivo diretamente, sem expor o path nem o ID.

## Abordagem: novo widget no mu-plugin, zero modificação no plugin

O widget `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` chamaria diretamente:

```php
$handler = Jet_Elements_Download_Handler::get_instance();
$hash    = $handler->encrypt_id( $attachment_id );
$url     = home_url( '/?jet_download=' . $hash );
```

Cada item do repeater teria um campo **Media** (attachment ID) em vez de URL manual. O dropdown renderiza com os links criptografados — o handler do JetElements intercepta automaticamente.

---

## Estrutura do widget revisada

**Tab Content:**

| Controle | Tipo | Detalhe |
|---|---|---|
| `label` | TEXT | Rótulo do botão (`DOWNLOAD`) |
| `links` | REPEATER | — |
| `links > item_label` | TEXT | Ex: `Português`, `English` |
| `links > item_file` | MEDIA | Seleciona da biblioteca de mídia |
| `links > item_fallback_url` | URL | Fallback para arquivos externos |

No render: se `item_file` tiver um ID → usa `encrypt_id()` → `?jet_download=HASH`. Se não tiver → cai no `item_fallback_url` (link direto, sem criptografia).

---

## Requisito

O widget verifica em runtime:

```php
if ( ! class_exists( 'Jet_Elements_Download_Handler' ) ) {
    // JetElements não está ativo — renderiza fallback simples
}
```

---

Quer que eu implemente agora? O mu-plugin existente (`bit-dropdown-btn.php`) seria atualizado para `v3.0.0` com essa lógica.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/dcambria/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.1/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec review loop** — dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent; fix issues and re-dispatch until approved (max 5 iterations, then surface to human)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec review loop" [shape=box];
    "Spec review passed?" [shape=diamond];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec review loop";
    "Spec review loop" -> "Spec review passed?";
    "Spec review passed?" -> "Spec review loop" [label="issues found,\nfix and re-dispatch"];
    "Spec review passed?" -> "User reviews spec?" [label="approved"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Review Loop:**
After writing the spec document:

1. Dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent (see spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md)
2. If Issues Found: fix, re-dispatch, repeat until Approved
3. If loop exceeds 5 iterations, surface to human for guidance

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

input.prompt:
In /Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/concertacao/wordpress/wp-content/, search for:

1. Any SVG icon related to "download" — look in themes/hello-elementor-child/svg/, and also any inline SVG definitions in style.css or functions.php with "download" or "ucpa-icon-download"
2. Read the style.css of the child theme to find the --ucpa-icon-download CSS variable definition (it should be a data URI SVG). Return the full value.
3. Also read the JetElements download button CSS at plugins/jet-elements/assets/css/addons/jet-download-button.css to understand what icon classes/styles it uses.
4. Read the JetElements download button widget PHP at plugins/jet-elements/includes/addons/jet-elements-download-button.php, specifically the get_icon() method and any icon-related controls.

Return all SVG data found, the full --ucpa-icon-download value if present, and the JetElements icon approach.

#!/bin/bash
# Decode the URL-encoded SVG from --ucpa-icon-download
encoded="%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='22' height='22' viewBox='0 0 22 22'%3E%3Cg%3E%3Cpath d='M11,0C4.92,0,0,4.92,0,11s4.92,11,11,11,11-4.92,11-11S17.08,0,11,0Zm.13,8.42h3.5l-1.75,3.03-1.75,3.03-1.75-3.03-1.75-3.03h3.5Z' /%3E%3C/g%3E%3C/svg%3E"
echo "$encoded" | python3 -c "import sys, urllib.parse; print(urllib.parse.unquote(sys.stdin.read()))"
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/decode-svg.sh

file.content:
# Visual Companion Guide Browser-based visual brainstorming companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and options. ## When to Use Decide per-question, not per-session. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?** **Use the browser** when the content itself is visual: - **UI mockups** — wireframes, layouts, navigation structures, component designs - **Architecture diagrams** — system components, data flow, relationship maps - **Side-by-side visual comparisons** — comparing two layouts, two color schemes, two design directions - **Design polish** — when the question is about look and feel, spacing, visual hierarchy - **Spatial relationships** — state machines, flowcharts, entity relationships rendered as diagrams **Use the terminal** when the content is text or tabular: - **Requirements and scope questions** — "what does X mean?", "which features are in scope?" - **Conceptual A/B/C choices** — picking between approaches described in words - **Tradeoff lists** — pros/cons, comparison tables - **Technical decisions** — API design, data modeling, architectural approach selection - **Clarifying questions** — anything where the answer is words, not a visual preference A question *about* a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What kind of wizard do you want?" is conceptual — use the terminal. "Which of these wizard layouts feels right?" is visual — use the browser. ## How It Works The server watches a directory for HTML files and serves the newest one to the browser. You write HTML content, the user sees it in their browser and can click to select options. Selections are recorded to a `.events` file that you read on your next turn. **Content fragments vs full documents:** If your HTML file starts with `/.superpowers/brainstorm/` for the session directory. **Note:** Pass the project root as `--project-dir` so mockups persist in `.superpowers/brainstorm/` and survive server restarts. Without it, files go to `/tmp` and get cleaned up. Remind the user to add `.superpowers/` to `.gitignore` if it's not already there. **Launching the server by platform:** **Claude Code:** ```bash # Default mode works — the script backgrounds the server itself scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project ``` **Codex:** ```bash # Codex reaps background processes. The script auto-detects CODEX_CI and # switches to foreground mode. Run it normally — no extra flags needed. scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project ``` **Gemini CLI:** ```bash # Use --foreground and set is_background: true on your shell tool call # so the process survives across turns scripts/start-server.sh --project-dir /path/to/project --foreground ``` **Other environments:** The server must keep running in the background across conversation turns. If your environment reaps detached processes, use `--foreground` and launch the command with your platform's background execution mechanism. If the URL is unreachable from your browser (common in remote/containerized setups), bind a non-loopback host: ```bash scripts/start-server.sh \ --project-dir /path/to/project \ --host 0.0.0.0 \ --url-host localhost ``` Use `--url-host` to control what hostname is printed in the returned URL JSON. ## The Loop 1. **Write HTML** to a new file in `screen_dir`: - Use semantic filenames: `platform.html`, `visual-style.html`, `layout.html` - **Never reuse filenames** — each screen gets a fresh file - Use Write tool — **never use cat/heredoc** (dumps noise into terminal) - Server automatically serves the newest file 2. **Tell user what to expect and end your turn:** - Remind them of the URL (every step, not just first) - Give a brief text summary of what's on screen (e.g., "Showing 3 layout options for the homepage") - Ask them to respond in the terminal: "Take a look and let me know what you think. Click to select an option if you'd like." 3. **On your next turn** — after the user responds in the terminal: - Read `$SCREEN_DIR/.events` if it exists — this contains the user's browser interactions (clicks, selections) as JSON lines - Merge with the user's terminal text to get the full picture - The terminal message is the primary feedback; `.events` provides structured interaction data 4. **Iterate or advance** — if feedback changes current screen, write a new file (e.g., `layout-v2.html`). Only move to the next question when the current step is validated. 5. **Unload when returning to terminal** — when the next step doesn't need the browser (e.g., a clarifying question, a tradeoff discussion), push a waiting screen to clear the stale content: ```html

Continuing in terminal...

``` This prevents the user from staring at a resolved choice while the conversation has moved on. When the next visual question comes up, push a new content file as usual. 6. Repeat until done. ## Writing Content Fragments Write just the content that goes inside the page. The server wraps it in the frame template automatically (header, theme CSS, selection indicator, and all interactive infrastructure). **Minimal example:** ```html

Which layout works better?

Consider readability and visual hierarchy

A

Single Column

Clean, focused reading experience

B

Two Column

Sidebar navigation with main content

``` That's it. No ``, no CSS, no `

content.text:
Todas as seções confirmadas. Aqui está o design completo para aprovação:

---

## Design — BIT Dropdown Button v3.0.0

### Arquivo
`mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` (versão atual v2.0.0 → v3.0.0)

### Criptografia
O widget chama `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler::get_instance()->encrypt_id($attachment_id)` para gerar `?jet_download=HASH`. O handler do JetElements já intercepta e serve o arquivo. Se o JetElements não estiver ativo, cai para o `item_fallback_url` sem criptografia.

### Tab Content

| Controle | Tipo | Detalhe |
|---|---|---|
| `label` | TEXT | Rótulo do botão, default `DOWNLOAD` |
| `links` (repeater) | REPEATER | — |
| `↳ item_label` | TEXT | Ex: `Português`, `English` |
| `↳ item_file` | MEDIA | Seleciona attachment da biblioteca |
| `↳ item_fallback_url` | URL | Usado quando não há attachment (externo) |

### Tab Style

**Botão — Dimensões**
- `btn_width` SLIDER (px/%)
- `btn_border_radius` DIMENSIONS
- `btn_padding` DIMENSIONS
- `btn_box_shadow` BOX_SHADOW

**Cores — Normal**
- `color_bg`, `color_txt`, `color_bdr` → CSS vars `--btn-normal-bg/txt/bdr`

**Cores — Hover**
- `color_bg_hv`, `color_txt_hv`, `color_bdr_hv` → CSS vars `--btn-normal-*-hv`

**Ícone**
- `icon` ICONS (picker nativo Elementor — default = SVG do círculo com seta)
- `icon_size` SLIDER (px)
- `color_icn`, `color_icn_hv` → CSS vars `--btn-normal-icn / --btn-normal-icon-hv`

**Tipografia**
- `label_typography` TYPOGRAPHY GROUP (label do botão)
- `menu_typography` TYPOGRAPHY GROUP (itens do dropdown)

**Menu Dropdown**
- `menu_min_width` SLIDER (px/%)
- `menu_item_padding` DIMENSIONS
- `menu_color_bg`, `menu_color_txt` → fundo e texto normais
- `menu_color_txt_hv`, `menu_color_bg_hv` → hover
- `menu_color_bdr`, `menu_color_divider` → borda e separador entre itens

### CSS
O `bit-dropdown-btn.css` recebe apenas ajustes para suportar os novos seletores (`border-radius`, `padding`, `box-shadow`, `min-width` do menu). O ícone continua inline via `render()`, agora via `Icons_Manager::render_icon()`.

### Render
```
div.dropdown-btn-wrapper
  div.dropdown-btn-container
    button.dropdown-btn-toggle
      div.dropdown-btn-content
        span.dropdown-btn-label   ← label
        span.dropdown-btn-icon    ← ícone via Icons_Manager
    div.dropdown-btn-menu
      a[href="?jet_download=HASH"] ← criptografado
      a[href="?jet_download=HASH"] ← criptografado
```

---

Tudo certo assim ou quer ajustar algum controle antes de eu escrever o plano?

input.content:
# Design: BIT Dropdown Button v3.0.0 **Data:** 2026-03-12 **Autor:** Bureau IT **Status:** Aprovado --- ## Contexto A página `/publicacoes/` usa botões "DOWNLOAD" com dropdown para múltiplos arquivos (PT/EN). O widget atual (v2.0.0) usa links diretos, expondo URLs de arquivos para rastreamento por buscadores. Esta versão integra a criptografia nativa do JetElements (plugin CrocoBl/CrocoBlock) para gerar URLs ofuscadas no formato `?jet_download=SHA1_HASH`, impedindo que buscadores rastreiem os links diretos de download. --- ## Arquivos Modificados | Arquivo | Ação | |---|---| | `wordpress/wp-content/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` | Atualizar de v2.0.0 → v3.0.0 | | `wordpress/wp-content/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.css` | Ajustes para novos seletores | | `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` | Espelho (cópia após alteração) | | `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.css` | Espelho (cópia após alteração) | --- ## Mecanismo de Criptografia O `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` (JetElements) armazena um mapa `SHA1_HASH → attachment_id` na opção WP `jet_elements_download_button_hashes`. A chave usa `NONCE_SALT`. ``` encrypt_id($id) → sha1(NONCE_SALT . $id) → salva mapa → retorna hash URL gerada: home_url('/?jet_download=' . $hash) ``` O handler intercepta no hook `init` (priority 99), decripta o hash, valida o attachment e serve o arquivo via headers HTTP com streaming em chunks de 1MB. **Fallback:** se `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` não estiver disponível (JetElements inativo), o widget usa `item_fallback_url` como link direto sem criptografia. --- ## Widget: `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Classe:** `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Slug:** `bit_dropdown_btn` **Título:** `BIT Download Button` **Ícone:** `eicon-button` **Categoria:** `general` ### Tab Content | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | |---|---|---|---| | Rótulo do botão | `label` | TEXT | `DOWNLOAD` | | Links (repeater) | `links` | REPEATER | 2 itens padrão | | ↳ Rótulo do item | `item_label` | TEXT | `Português` | | ↳ Arquivo (media) | `item_file` | MEDIA | — | | ↳ URL fallback | `item_fallback_url` | URL | — | **Lógica de URL por item:** 1. Se `item_file` tem ID → `encrypt_id(id)` → `?jet_download=HASH` 2. Senão → `item_fallback_url` (link direto) 3. Se nenhum → item não renderizado ### Tab Style #### Seção: Botão — Dimensões | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor CSS | |---|---|---|---| | Largura | `btn_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Border radius | `btn_border_radius` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { border-radius: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding | `btn_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { padding: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Box shadow | `btn_box_shadow` | BOX_SHADOW | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle` | #### Seção: Cores — Normal | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo | `color_bg` | `--btn-normal-bg` | | Texto | `color_txt` | `--btn-normal-txt` | | Borda | `color_bdr` | `--btn-normal-bdr` | #### Seção: Cores — Hover | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo hover | `color_bg_hv` | `--btn-normal-bg-hv` | | Texto hover | `color_txt_hv` | `--btn-normal-txt-hv` | | Borda hover | `color_bdr_hv` | `--btn-normal-border-hv` | #### Seção: Ícone | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor/Var | |---|---|---|---| | Ícone | `icon` | ICONS | renderizado via `Icons_Manager::render_icon()` | | Tamanho | `icon_size` | SLIDER (px) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-icon svg { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; height: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Cor normal | `color_icn` | COLOR | `--btn-normal-icn` | | Cor hover | `color_icn_hv` | COLOR | `--btn-normal-icon-hv` | **Default do ícone:** SVG inline do círculo com seta (path do `--ucpa-icon-download`) pré-configurado como default no controle ICONS via `skin: 'caret'` ou SVG embutido como valor padrão. #### Seção: Tipografia | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor | |---|---|---|---| | Label botão | `label_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-label` | | Itens menu | `menu_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a` | #### Seção: Menu Dropdown | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor/Var | |---|---|---|---| | Largura mínima | `menu_min_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { min-width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding item | `menu_item_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { padding: ... }` | | Fundo | `menu_color_bg` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { background-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto | `menu_color_txt` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto hover | `menu_color_txt_hv` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a:hover { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Fundo hover | `menu_color_bg_hv` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a:hover { background-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor borda | `menu_color_bdr` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { border-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor separador | `menu_color_divider` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { border-bottom-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | --- ## Render HTML ```html
Português English
``` --- ## CSS — Ajustes em `bit-dropdown-btn.css` - Remover regras `width: 220px` hardcoded do `.dropdown-btn-toggle` (passa a ser controlado via Elementor) - Remover `padding: 15px 25px` hardcoded (idem) - Remover `border-radius: 4px` hardcoded onde sobrepõe o controle Elementor - Adicionar: `.dropdown-btn-icon svg { display: block; }` para normalizar SVG inline - Manter: hover, dropdown menu, transições --- ## Verificação 1. No Elementor, buscar "BIT Download Button" → widget aparece 2. Inserir widget → Tab Content: configurar label + 2 links com attachments da mídia 3. Verificar URLs geradas: devem ser `?jet_download=HASH` (não IDs diretos) 4. Hover: dropdown abre, cores mudam, ícone muda de cor 5. Tab Style → todos os controles refletem no preview 6. Controle Typography → tipografia aplica nos seletores corretos 7. Download efetivo: clicar em link → arquivo serve corretamente 8. Fallback: remover attachment, preencher só `item_fallback_url` → link direto funciona 9. JetElements inativo: widget renderiza com fallback URL sem erro fatal

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the review.

---

## ISSUES FOUND

The spec is well-structured and the encryption mechanism description is accurate. However, there are several issues ranging from critical to suggestions that must be addressed before implementation.

---

### CRITICAL

**1. Wrong method called for URL generation**

The spec (Section "Mecanismo de Criptografia" and "Lógica de URL por item") instructs calling `encrypt_id($id)` directly, but that method is declared `private` in `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler`. The correct public API is `get_download_link( $id )`, which internally calls `encrypt_id()` and returns the full URL via `add_query_arg`. The implementation code must call:

```php
jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id )
```

Not a hand-rolled `home_url('/?jet_download=' . encrypt_id($id))`. If the spec is treated literally, implementation will fail with a fatal error or require reflection hacks.

The spec must either: (a) update the "Lógica de URL" table to reference `get_download_link()`, or (b) explicitly note that `get_download_link()` is the entry point and the internal `encrypt_id` call is just illustrative of the mechanism.

**2. JetElements availability check is incomplete**

The spec states: "se `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` não estiver disponível… usa `item_fallback_url`". But the handler is a singleton that must also have been initialized (its `init()` method called) to register the `process_download` hook. The class existing does not guarantee the handler is active. The correct check is:

```php
function_exists( 'jet_elements_download_handler' ) && jet_elements_download_handler() instanceof Jet_Elements_Download_Handler
```

The spec should specify which condition gates the fallback, or at minimum note that the function `jet_elements_download_handler()` must exist.

**3. MEDIA control does not return a simple ID — attachment sub-key required**

The spec Table (Tab Content) shows `item_file` as type MEDIA with no note about how to read the attachment ID. Elementor's `MEDIA` control returns an array: `[ 'id' => int, 'url' => string ]`. The render logic must access `$link['item_file']['id']` (not `$link['item_file']`). Without this note, an implementer could write a direct cast to `(int)$link['item_file']` which always yields 0 and always triggers the fallback. The spec must document this explicitly in the "Lógica de URL por item" section.

---

### IMPORTANT

**4. Icon control default value: vague and likely unimplementable**

Section "Seção: Ícone" states: `Default do ícone: SVG inline do círculo com seta... pré-configurado como default no controle ICONS via skin: 'caret' ou SVG embutido como valor padrão.`

The `ICONS` control in Elementor does not accept an SVG string as its default value. The `default` key accepts a value structure like `['value' => 'eicon-download', 'library' => 'eicons']`. The v2 implementation avoids this entirely by using a private `get_download_icon_svg()` method that renders a hardcoded SVG when the ICONS control has no selection.

The spec must clarify: should the widget keep the same pattern as v2 (private hardcoded SVG as fallback when the ICONS control is empty), or should it use a specific Elementor icon library icon as default? The phrase "SVG embutido como valor padrão" is ambiguous and will lead to either an incorrect implementation or wasted time.

**5. CSS spec says to remove `width: 220px` and `border-radius: 4px` but v2 uses them at specificity (0,2,1)**

The existing CSS rule `.dropdown-btn-container button.dropdown-btn-toggle` uses specificity (0,2,1), which is documented inline in the CSS file itself. The Elementor `selectors` output generates rules on `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle` which is approximately (0,1,1). If the hardcoded values are removed but the Elementor control is left empty/zero (no value set), the button loses its dimensions entirely. The spec must state what default values the Elementor controls should carry (`btn_width` default: 220px is already in v2, but `btn_border_radius` and `btn_padding` have no defaults specified). Add default values for `btn_border_radius` (4px) and `btn_padding` (15px 25px) to the Tab Style control table.

**6. `color_bdr_hv` CSS variable name inconsistency**

In the "Seção: Cores — Hover" table, `color_bdr_hv` maps to `--btn-normal-border-hv`. But in "Seção: Cores — Normal", `color_bdr` maps to `--btn-normal-bdr`. The naming pattern is inconsistent (`-bdr` vs `-border-`). The existing CSS (line 8 of `bit-dropdown-btn.css`) and v2 code already use `--btn-normal-border-hv`. This inconsistency between the two hover color var names should be acknowledged explicitly in the spec so the implementer does not "fix" the existing variable name and break the CSS.

**7. Repeater default items: field name mismatch**

The v2 default for the repeater's URL field is `'item_url'`. The v3 spec renames this field to `'item_file'` (MEDIA) + `'item_fallback_url'` (URL). The spec must include the updated `default` array for the `links` repeater control with the new field keys, since copy-pasting from v2 will silently use the wrong keys and render no URLs.

---

### SUGGESTIONS

**8. Verification step 9 is not testable as written**

"JetElements inativo: widget renderiza com fallback URL sem erro fatal" — in a mu-plugins context, the check `class_exists('Jet_Elements_Download_Handler')` happens at render time. The spec should clarify how to simulate this state locally (deactivate JetElements plugin) and what the expected output is (renders `item_fallback_url` as `<a href>`, or skips item entirely if both are empty).

**9. No `nofollow` attribute specified for encrypted download links**

The render HTML example shows `rel="noopener"` only. For download URLs the spec should clarify whether `rel="nofollow"` should also be added, since the purpose of the encryption is explicitly to prevent search engine crawling. The fallback URL path already had `nofollow` support in v2.

**10. No mention of the `common/mu-plugins/` mirror step in the implementation workflow**

Per `/Users/dcambria/scripts/server-tools/v2/docker-dev/sites/CLAUDE.md`, every mu-plugin change must be copied to `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/` and committed in the server-tools repo. The spec's "Arquivos Modificados" table lists this, which is correct — but there is no corresponding verification step in Section "Verificação" confirming the mirror was done. Add a step 10 to the verification checklist.

---

### Summary

| Severity | Count | Items |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | 3 | #1 wrong method, #2 incomplete availability check, #3 MEDIA control sub-key |
| Important | 4 | #4 icon default ambiguity, #5 missing control defaults, #6 CSS var naming inconsistency, #7 repeater default field keys |
| Suggestions | 3 | #8 untestable step, #9 nofollow for encrypted links, #10 missing mirror verification step |

The spec requires corrections to items #1 through #7 before implementation can proceed safely. Items #1 and #3 in particular will cause silent runtime failures — the download URL will never be generated — unless the spec is clarified.

O widget atual (v2.0.0) usa links diretos, expondo URLs de arquivos para rastreamento por buscadores. Esta versão integra a criptografia nativa do JetElements (plugin CrocoBl/CrocoBlock) para gerar URLs ofuscadas no formato `?jet_download=SHA1_HASH`, impedindo que buscadores rastreiem os links diretos de download. --- ## Arquivos Modificados | Arquivo | Ação | |---|---| | `wordpress/wp-content/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` | Atualizar de v2.0.0 → v3.0.0 | | `wordpress/wp-content/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.css` | Ajustes para novos seletores | | `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` | Espelho (cópia após alteração) | | `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.css` | Espelho (cópia após alteração) | --- ## Mecanismo de Criptografia O `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` (JetElements) armazena um mapa `SHA1_HASH → attachment_id` na opção WP `jet_elements_download_button_hashes`. A chave usa `NONCE_SALT`. **API pública correta (único ponto de entrada):** ```php // Gera a URL completa com hash — não chamar encrypt_id() diretamente (método private) jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id ) // Retorna: "https://site.com/?jet_download=abc123sha1hash" ``` O handler intercepta no hook `init` (priority 99), decripta o hash, valida o attachment e serve o arquivo via headers HTTP com streaming em chunks de 1MB. **Verificação de disponibilidade:** ```php // Condição correta — class_exists() sozinho não garante que o handler foi inicializado if ( function_exists( 'jet_elements_download_handler' ) && jet_elements_download_handler() instanceof Jet_Elements_Download_Handler ) { $url = jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id ); } else { $url = $item_fallback_url; // link direto sem criptografia } ``` --- ## Widget: `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Classe:** `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Slug:** `bit_dropdown_btn` **Título:** `BIT Download Button` **Ícone:** `eicon-button` **Categoria:** `general` ### Tab Content | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | |---|---|---|---| | Rótulo do botão | `label` | TEXT | `DOWNLOAD` | | Links (repeater) | `links` | REPEATER | 2 itens padrão (ver abaixo) | | ↳ Rótulo do item | `item_label` | TEXT | `Português` / `English` | | ↳ Arquivo (media) | `item_file` | MEDIA | `['id' => 0, 'url' => '']` | | ↳ URL fallback | `item_fallback_url` | URL | `['url' => '']` | **Default do repeater (campo `default` no controle `links`):** ```php 'default' => [ [ 'item_label' => 'Português', 'item_file' => [ 'id' => 0, 'url' => '' ], 'item_fallback_url' => [ 'url' => '' ] ], [ 'item_label' => 'English', 'item_file' => [ 'id' => 0, 'url' => '' ], 'item_fallback_url' => [ 'url' => '' ] ], ], ``` **Lógica de URL por item no `render()`:** O controle MEDIA retorna array `['id' => int, 'url' => string]`. Acessar sempre via `$link['item_file']['id']`. ```php $attachment_id = (int) ( $link['item_file']['id'] ?? 0 ); $fallback_url = $link['item_fallback_url']['url'] ?? ''; if ( $attachment_id > 0 && function_exists( 'jet_elements_download_handler' ) && jet_elements_download_handler() instanceof Jet_Elements_Download_Handler ) { $href = jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id ); } elseif ( ! empty( $fallback_url ) ) { $href = esc_url( $fallback_url ); } else { continue; // item sem URL válida — não renderiza } ``` ### Tab Style #### Seção: Botão — Dimensões | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | Seletor CSS | |---|---|---|---|---| | Largura | `btn_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | `220px` | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Border radius | `btn_border_radius` | DIMENSIONS | `4px` (todos lados) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { border-radius: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding | `btn_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `15px` top/bottom, `25px` left/right | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { padding: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Box shadow | `btn_box_shadow` | BOX_SHADOW | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle` | #### Seção: Cores — Normal | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo | `color_bg` | `--btn-normal-bg` | | Texto | `color_txt` | `--btn-normal-txt` | | Borda | `color_bdr` | `--btn-normal-bdr` | #### Seção: Cores — Hover | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo hover | `color_bg_hv` | `--btn-normal-bg-hv` | | Texto hover | `color_txt_hv` | `--btn-normal-txt-hv` | | Borda hover | `color_bdr_hv` | `--btn-normal-border-hv` (manter nome existente — inconsistência herdada do CSS) | #### Seção: Ícone | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | Seletor/Var | |---|---|---|---|---| | Ícone | `icon` | ICONS | `['value' => 'eicon-download', 'library' => 'eicons']` | renderizado via `Icons_Manager::render_icon()` | | Tamanho | `icon_size` | SLIDER (px) | `30px` | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-icon svg, {{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-icon i { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; height: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Cor normal | `color_icn` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-icn: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor hover | `color_icn_hv` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} { --btn-normal-icon-hv: {{VALUE}}; }` | **Fallback de ícone:** quando o controle `icon` estiver vazio ou não retornar HTML, renderizar o SVG hardcoded do círculo com seta (mesmo padrão da v2, via método privado `get_default_icon_svg()`). Isso garante que o botão sempre exibe um ícone sem depender da seleção do usuário. O SVG padrão (do `--ucpa-icon-download`): ```php private function get_default_icon_svg(): string { return ''; } ``` #### Seção: Tipografia | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor | |---|---|---|---| | Label botão | `label_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-label` | | Itens menu | `menu_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a` | #### Seção: Menu Dropdown | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | Seletor | |---|---|---|---|---| | Largura mínima | `menu_min_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { min-width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding item | `menu_item_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `12px` top/bottom, `20px` left/right | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { padding: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Fundo | `menu_color_bg` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { background-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto | `menu_color_txt` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto hover | `menu_color_txt_hv` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a:hover { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Fundo hover | `menu_color_bg_hv` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a:hover { background-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor borda | `menu_color_bdr` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { border-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor separador | `menu_color_divider` | COLOR | — | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { border-bottom-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | --- ## Render HTML ```html
Português English
``` **Atributos `rel`:** - Links criptografados (`?jet_download=`): `rel="nofollow noopener"` — o objetivo explícito é impedir rastreamento por buscadores - Links fallback (URL direta): `rel="nofollow noopener"` igualmente - Se `item_fallback_url` tiver `is_external: true`: adicionar também `target="_blank"` --- ## CSS — Ajustes em `bit-dropdown-btn.css` Remover regras hardcoded que serão substituídas pelos seletores Elementor (o controle com `default` garante que o valor sempre existe): - `width: 220px` em `.dropdown-btn-container button.dropdown-btn-toggle` - `padding: 15px 25px` no mesmo seletor - `border-radius: 4px` nas regras normal e hover do toggle (manter apenas o `border-radius: 4px 4px 0 0` do hover que é lógico de layout) Adicionar: - `.dropdown-btn-icon svg, .dropdown-btn-icon i { display: block; width: 100%; height: 100%; }` — normaliza tanto SVG inline quanto `` de Font Awesome --- ## Verificação 1. No Elementor, buscar "BIT Download Button" → widget aparece na barra lateral 2. Inserir widget → Tab Content: configurar label + 2 links com attachments da biblioteca de mídia 3. Verificar URLs geradas no HTML: devem conter `?jet_download=` (não IDs numéricos diretos) 4. Clicar em link de download → arquivo serve corretamente (sem 404) 5. Hover: dropdown abre, cores mudam, ícone muda de cor 6. Tab Style → todos os controles refletem no preview do Elementor 7. Controle Typography → tipografia aplica nos seletores corretos 8. Fallback: deixar `item_file` vazio, preencher só `item_fallback_url` → link direto funciona com `rel="nofollow noopener"` 9. JetElements inativo: desativar plugin JetElements → widget renderiza usando `item_fallback_url` sem erro fatal (verificar que não há `Call to undefined function jet_elements_download_handler()`) 10. Mirror: confirmar que `docker-dev/common/mu-plugins/bit-dropdown-btn.php` e `.css` foram copiados e commitados no repo server-tools

A chave usa `NONCE_SALT`. ``` encrypt_id($id) → sha1(NONCE_SALT . $id) → salva mapa → retorna hash URL gerada: home_url('/?jet_download=' . $hash) ``` O handler intercepta no hook `init` (priority 99), decripta o hash, valida o attachment e serve o arquivo via headers HTTP com streaming em chunks de 1MB. **Fallback:** se `Jet_Elements_Download_Handler` não estiver disponível (JetElements inativo), o widget usa `item_fallback_url` como link direto sem criptografia. --- ## Widget: `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Classe:** `Bit_Dropdown_Btn_Widget` **Slug:** `bit_dropdown_btn` **Título:** `BIT Download Button` **Ícone:** `eicon-button` **Categoria:** `general` ### Tab Content | Controle | ID | Tipo | Default | |---|---|---|---| | Rótulo do botão | `label` | TEXT | `DOWNLOAD` | | Links (repeater) | `links` | REPEATER | 2 itens padrão | | ↳ Rótulo do item | `item_label` | TEXT | `Português` | | ↳ Arquivo (media) | `item_file` | MEDIA | — | | ↳ URL fallback | `item_fallback_url` | URL | — | **Lógica de URL por item:** 1. Se `item_file` tem ID → `encrypt_id(id)` → `?jet_download=HASH` 2. Senão → `item_fallback_url` (link direto) 3. Se nenhum → item não renderizado ### Tab Style #### Seção: Botão — Dimensões | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor CSS | |---|---|---|---| | Largura | `btn_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Border radius | `btn_border_radius` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { border-radius: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding | `btn_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle { padding: {{TOP}}{{UNIT}} {{RIGHT}}{{UNIT}} {{BOTTOM}}{{UNIT}} {{LEFT}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Box shadow | `btn_box_shadow` | BOX_SHADOW | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-toggle` | #### Seção: Cores — Normal | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo | `color_bg` | `--btn-normal-bg` | | Texto | `color_txt` | `--btn-normal-txt` | | Borda | `color_bdr` | `--btn-normal-bdr` | #### Seção: Cores — Hover | Controle | ID | CSS var | |---|---|---| | Fundo hover | `color_bg_hv` | `--btn-normal-bg-hv` | | Texto hover | `color_txt_hv` | `--btn-normal-txt-hv` | | Borda hover | `color_bdr_hv` | `--btn-normal-border-hv` | #### Seção: Ícone | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor/Var | |---|---|---|---| | Ícone | `icon` | ICONS | renderizado via `Icons_Manager::render_icon()` | | Tamanho | `icon_size` | SLIDER (px) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-icon svg { width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; height: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Cor normal | `color_icn` | COLOR | `--btn-normal-icn` | | Cor hover | `color_icn_hv` | COLOR | `--btn-normal-icon-hv` | **Default do ícone:** SVG inline do círculo com seta (path do `--ucpa-icon-download`) pré-configurado como default no controle ICONS via `skin: 'caret'` ou SVG embutido como valor padrão. #### Seção: Tipografia | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor | |---|---|---|---| | Label botão | `label_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-label` | | Itens menu | `menu_typography` | TYPOGRAPHY | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a` | #### Seção: Menu Dropdown | Controle | ID | Tipo | Seletor/Var | |---|---|---|---| | Largura mínima | `menu_min_width` | SLIDER (px/%) | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { min-width: {{SIZE}}{{UNIT}}; }` | | Padding item | `menu_item_padding` | DIMENSIONS | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { padding: ... }` | | Fundo | `menu_color_bg` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu { background-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto | `menu_color_txt` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Texto hover | `menu_color_txt_hv` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a:hover { color: {{VALUE}}; }` | | Cor separador | `menu_color_divider` | COLOR | `{{WRAPPER}} .dropdown-btn-menu a { border-bottom-color: {{VALUE}}; }` | --- ## Render HTML ```html
Português English
``` --- ## CSS — Ajustes em `bit-dropdown-btn.css` - Remover regras `width: 220px` hardcoded do `.dropdown-btn-toggle` (passa a ser controlado via Elementor) - Remover `padding: 15px 25px` hardcoded (idem) - Remover `border-radius: 4px` hardcoded onde sobrepõe o controle Elementor - Adicionar: `.dropdown-btn-icon svg { display: block; }` para normalizar SVG inline - Manter: hover, dropdown menu, transições --- ## Verificação 1. No Elementor, buscar "BIT Download Button" → widget aparece 2. Inserir widget → Tab Content: configurar label + 2 links com attachments da mídia 3. Verificar URLs geradas: devem ser `?jet_download=HASH` (não IDs diretos) 4. Hover: dropdown abre, cores mudam, ícone muda de cor 5. Tab Style → todos os controles refletem no preview 6. Controle Typography → tipografia aplica nos seletores corretos 7. Download efetivo: clicar em link → arquivo serve corretamente 8. Fallback: remover attachment, preencher só `item_fallback_url` → link direto funciona 9. JetElements inativo: widget renderiza com fallback URL sem erro fatal

content.text:
The spec has been fully read. Now I will verify each of the 7 previously reported issues against the current document.

**Issue 1 — Wrong method (`encrypt_id` private → `get_download_link` public)**

Line 36: `jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id )` is correctly specified, with an explicit inline comment on line 35 saying "não chamar `encrypt_id()` diretamente (método private)". Fixed.

**Issue 2 — Incomplete JetElements availability check**

Lines 46–51: The guard now uses `function_exists( 'jet_elements_download_handler' ) && jet_elements_download_handler() instanceof Jet_Elements_Download_Handler`, with a prose comment explaining why `class_exists()` alone is insufficient. The same double-guard is reproduced in the render logic on lines 91–93. Fixed.

**Issue 3 — MEDIA control sub-key (`item_file['id']`)**

Line 84 explicitly states "Acessar sempre via `$link['item_file']['id']`". Line 87 shows `(int) ( $link['item_file']['id'] ?? 0 )`. Table row at line 71 documents the MEDIA default as `['id' => 0, 'url' => '']`. Fixed.

**Issue 4 — Icon default ambiguity**

Line 132: `icon` control default is `['value' => 'eicon-download', 'library' => 'eicons']` — both keys present, unambiguous. Fixed.

**Issue 5 — Missing control defaults (`border_radius`, `padding`)**

Line 108: `btn_border_radius` DIMENSIONS default documented as `4px (todos lados)`.
Line 109: `btn_padding` DIMENSIONS default documented as `15px top/bottom, 25px left/right`. Fixed.

**Issue 6 — CSS var naming inconsistency (`--btn-normal-border-hv`)**

Line 126: The name `--btn-normal-border-hv` is retained with an explicit parenthetical annotation: "(manter nome existente — inconsistência herdada do CSS)". The spec now acknowledges the inconsistency and gives a clear implementation instruction. Fixed.

**Issue 7 — Repeater default field keys**

Lines 76–79: The repeater `default` array uses the exact control IDs `item_label`, `item_file`, `item_fallback_url` with correctly structured sub-arrays including both `['id' => 0, 'url' => '']` for the MEDIA control and `['url' => '']` for the URL control. Fixed.

**Additional pass — checking for new issues**

One remaining concern worth flagging as a suggestion: the `item_fallback_url` URL control default in the repeater is `['url' => '']`, but the render logic on line 95 applies `esc_url( $fallback_url )` where `$fallback_url` is already a raw URL string (extracted from the array on line 88). This is correct. No inconsistency here.

The `get_default_icon_svg()` SVG (lines 141–147) declares `fill="currentColor"` at the root element but the path data describes a shape with an interior cutout. `fill="currentColor"` on the `<svg>` element combined with a single `<path>` with an implicit `fill-rule: nonzero` will fill the cutout. If the intent is a donut/hollow arrow shape, the spec should specify `fill-rule="evenodd"` on the path. This is a new minor issue not present in the previous review.

The `is_external` check on line 198 is referenced for `item_fallback_url`, but the control table (line 72) defines it only as type URL with no mention of an `is_external` toggle. Elementor's URL control does include that toggle by default, but since no explicit control configuration disabling it is shown, this is consistent. Not an issue.

---

**ISSUES FOUND**

All 7 previously reported issues have been resolved. One new minor issue was identified:

**Suggestion — SVG fill-rule for hollow icon (line 145–146)**

The default icon SVG uses `fill="currentColor"` at the SVG level with a single path that appears to describe a filled disc with a triangle punched out (`fill-rule: nonzero` applies by default). If the visual intent is a hollow interior (the downward arrow is the negative space within a filled circle), the path requires `fill-rule="evenodd"` on the `<path>` element; otherwise the "hole" fills solid. This is low-risk since the SVG is a private method and can be verified visually immediately, but the spec should document the intended fill-rule to avoid ambiguity during implementation.

Corrected line:
```php
// In get_default_icon_svg(), change:
'<path d="M11,0C4.92,0...">'
// to:
'<path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M11,0C4.92,0...">'
```

**Severity: Suggestion** — does not block implementation, but should be validated at the visual verification step (step 5 of the verification checklist at line 225).

All critical and important issues from the prior review are closed. The spec is implementation-ready pending this one clarification.

The spec has been fully read. Now I will verify each of the 7 previously reported issues against the current document.

**Issue 1 — Wrong method (`encrypt_id` private → `get_download_link` public)**

Line 36: `jet_elements_download_handler()->get_download_link( $attachment_id )` is correctly specified, with an explicit inline comment on line 35 saying "não chamar `encrypt_id()` diretamente (método private)". Fixed.

**Issue 2 — Incomplete JetElements availability check**

Lines 46–51: The guard now uses `function_exists( 'jet_elements_download_handler' ) && jet_elements_download_handler() instanceof Jet_Elements_Download_Handler`, with a prose comment explaining why `class_exists()` alone is insufficient. The same double-guard is reproduced in the render logic on lines 91–93. Fixed.

**Issue 3 — MEDIA control sub-key (`item_file['id']`)**

Line 84 explicitly states "Acessar sempre via `$link['item_file']['id']`". Line 87 shows `(int) ( $link['item_file']['id'] ?? 0 )`. Table row at line 71 documents the MEDIA default as `['id' => 0, 'url' => '']`. Fixed.

**Issue 4 — Icon default ambiguity**

Line 132: `icon` control default is `['value' => 'eicon-download', 'library' => 'eicons']` — both keys present, unambiguous. Fixed.

**Issue 5 — Missing control defaults (`border_radius`, `padding`)**

Line 108: `btn_border_radius` DIMENSIONS default documented as `4px (todos lados)`.
Line 109: `btn_padding` DIMENSIONS default documented as `15px top/bottom, 25px left/right`. Fixed.

**Issue 6 — CSS var naming inconsistency (`--btn-normal-border-hv`)**

Line 126: The name `--btn-normal-border-hv` is retained with an explicit parenthetical annotation: "(manter nome existente — inconsistência herdada do CSS)". The spec now acknowledges the inconsistency and gives a clear implementation instruction. Fixed.

**Issue 7 — Repeater default field keys**

Lines 76–79: The repeater `default` array uses the exact control IDs `item_label`, `item_file`, `item_fallback_url` with correctly structured sub-arrays including both `['id' => 0, 'url' => '']` for the MEDIA control and `['url' => '']` for the URL control. Fixed.

**Additional pass — checking for new issues**

One remaining concern worth flagging as a suggestion: the `item_fallback_url` URL control default in the repeater is `['url' => '']`, but the render logic on line 95 applies `esc_url( $fallback_url )` where `$fallback_url` is already a raw URL string (extracted from the array on line 88). This is correct. No inconsistency here.

The `get_default_icon_svg()` SVG (lines 141–147) declares `fill="currentColor"` at the root element but the path data describes a shape with an interior cutout. `fill="currentColor"` on the `<svg>` element combined with a single `<path>` with an implicit `fill-rule: nonzero` will fill the cutout. If the intent is a donut/hollow arrow shape, the spec should specify `fill-rule="evenodd"` on the path. This is a new minor issue not present in the previous review.

The `is_external` check on line 198 is referenced for `item_fallback_url`, but the control table (line 72) defines it only as type URL with no mention of an `is_external` toggle. Elementor's URL control does include that toggle by default, but since no explicit control configuration disabling it is shown, this is consistent. Not an issue.

---

**ISSUES FOUND**

All 7 previously reported issues have been resolved. One new minor issue was identified:

**Suggestion — SVG fill-rule for hollow icon (line 145–146)**

The default icon SVG uses `fill="currentColor"` at the SVG level with a single path that appears to describe a filled disc with a triangle punched out (`fill-rule: nonzero` applies by default). If the visual intent is a hollow interior (the downward arrow is the negative space within a filled circle), the path requires `fill-rule="evenodd"` on the `<path>` element; otherwise the "hole" fills solid. This is low-risk since the SVG is a private method and can be verified visually immediately, but the spec should document the intended fill-rule to avoid ambiguity during implementation.

Corrected line:
```php
// In get_default_icon_svg(), change:
'<path d="M11,0C4.92,0...">'
// to:
'<path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M11,0C4.92,0...">'
```

**Severity: Suggestion** — does not block implementation, but should be validated at the visual verification step (step 5 of the verification checklist at line 225).

All critical and important issues from the prior review are closed. The spec is implementation-ready pending this one clarification.
Aprovado. Um ajuste final — `fill-rule="evenodd"` no path do SVG para garantir o interior vazado correto.
